title: William Blake
content:
He came to the desert of London town
    Gray miles long;
    He wandered up and he wandered down,
    Singing a quiet song.
    He came to the desert of London town,
    Mirk miles broad;
    He wandered up and he wandered down,
    Ever alone with God.
    There were thousands and thousands of human kind
    In this desert of brick and stone;
    But some were deaf and some were blind,
    And he was there alone.
    At length the good hour came; he died
    As he had lived, alone.
    He was not missed from the desert wide;
    Perhaps he was found at the throne.
meaning:
"William Blake" is a quintessential example of James Thomson -  (Bysshe Vanolis)'s signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
